content: Vaccine effectiveness interpretation challenges: healthy user bias (vaccinated individuals differ systematically from unvaccinated), confounding by indication (high-risk groups prioritized), misclassification of vaccination status or outcomes, and changing case definitions. Effectiveness varies by outcome: higher against severe disease/hospitalization than against infection. Variant-specific effectiveness requires genomic surveillance linkage. Time-varying effectiveness reflects waning immunity or immune escape variants. Confidence intervals indicate precision; overlapping CIs don't necessarily mean no difference. Real-world effectiveness informs booster timing and target populations.
source: Vaccine journal, CDC ACIP evidence frameworks
